channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pretoria court sentences man to 30 years for violent robberies targeting e-hailing drivers
item.description: A 39-year-old man from Eersterust, Kamar Davids, has been sentenced to 30 years in prison after being convicted of two violent robberies targeting e-hailing drivers in Pretoria. The Pretoria magistrat...

item.title: Snøhetta's Roosevelt Library Blends Seamlessly with the Natural Landscape
item.description: Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library, designed by Snøhetta, is a 95,000-square-foot structure integrated into the North Dakota Badlands. The building's design mimics natural formations, with a gree...

item.title: CIT University graduate leads in July 2026 master plumbers licensure exam
item.description: A graduate of the Cebu Institute of Technology-University (CIT-U) achieved the top score in the July 2026 master plumbers licensure examination, according to the Professional Regulation Commission (PR...

item.title: Greylock's Strategic Fund Capping at $1.5B to Focus on Early-Stage Investments
item.description: Greylock Ventures, a prestigious Silicon Valley venture firm, has raised a $1.5 billion 18th fund, intentionally capping the size to maintain a focused approach. Despite the potential to raise signifi...

item.title: Physiotherapist Mary Habila's Death Under Investigation After Being Found Dead At Minister's Residence
item.description: The family of 26-year-old physiotherapist Mary Habila has announced burial arrangements for the young professional who was found dead under controversial circumstances at Nigeria's Minister of Works, ...

item.title: Ariana Grande’s Harry Potter Impressions Spark Fan Admiration
item.description: Ariana Grande has released a video showcasing her impressions of iconic Harry Potter characters, including Molly Weasley, Dobby, Severus Snape, and Hermione Granger. The video, posted on Instagram as ...

item.title: Senate Calls for Immediate Security Measures After Killings in Benue Communities
item.description: The Nigerian Senate has passed a motion calling for immediate security measures to address recent killings in Benue State communities. The resolution, adopted unanimously during a plenary session chai...

item.title: Trump administration faces backlash over ICE fatalities amid political tensions
item.description: Tom Homan, the US border czar under President Donald Trump, has warned of escalating violence at the US-Mexico border unless Democratic lawmakers cease criticizing Immigration and Customs Enforcement ...

item.title: Argentina advances to World Cup final after thrilling 2-1 victory over England
item.description: Argentina secured their place in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final with a dramatic 2-1 victory over England in a tightly contested semi-final at the Atlanta Stadium. The match, which saw England take an e...

item.title: Property Market Outlook: Experts Predict Value Drops and Advise Buyers to Act Now
item.description: The article discusses current trends in Australia's property market, highlighting expert predictions that 78% of professionals expect residential property values to decline due to legislative reforms ...

item.title: Supreme Court expresses concern over judicial rulings on grooming cases
item.description: The article discusses the Supreme Court of India's expressed concerns regarding the Patna High Court's recent ruling on a grooming case, which has drawn criticism for potentially setting a precedent. ...

item.title: Minister Confident Tinubu Will Secure Strong Victory in 2027 Elections
item.description: Alhaji Uba Maigari, Minister of State for Regional Development, has expressed confidence that President Bola Tinubu's re-election bid in the 2027 general elections is unstoppable. During an APC stakeh...

item.title: Youth Seriously Injured in Chilliwack Assault, Charges Laid
item.description: A youth was seriously injured in an assault incident in Chilliwack, British Columbia, leading to charges being laid against the perpetrator. The incident, reported on July 15, 2026, involved a violent...

item.title: Tanga Bus Operators Dispute Relocation to Kange Terminal
item.description: A dispute has emerged in Tanga between city authorities, bus operators, and passengers over the relocation of regional bus services from the city centre to the Kange Regional Bus Terminal. Operators a...

item.title: US-Iran tensions escalate with military strikes and threats of regional conflict
item.description: The article details the escalating tensions between the United States and Iran following a series of military strikes by the US on Iran's coastal defenses and missile sites. The attacks, which occurre...

item.title: Bengal politician records voice sample amid legal probe into election remarks

item.title: WAGs redefining their role in football: From fashion icons to supportive partners
item.description: The article explores the evolving role of WAGs (Wives and Girlfriends of Footballers) in modern football, focusing on how they are moving beyond traditional stereotypes. It highlights the influence of...

item.title: Stakeholder input on public procurement reforms aims to enhance economic transformation
item.description: Stakeholders have called for a more decentralised and responsive public procurement system to drive economic transformation in South Africa. The joint submission to the National Treasury highlights th...

item.title: 1947 Chrysler Town and Country Sedan: A Postwar Classic with Wood-Bodied Elegance
item.description: The 1947 Chrysler Town and Country Sedan represents a pinnacle of postwar American automotive design, blending luxury with practicality. This wood-bodied station wagon, part of Chrysler's signature 'w...

item.title: Bangsamoro Implements Gun Ban Ahead of Historic Parliamentary Elections
item.description: A gun ban has been enforced starting July 16, 2026, across the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), marking the official start of the election period for the region's first parliam...
